title: Webhook key comparison via constant-time SHA-256 hash equality
description: In webhook.rs lines 54-58, the webhook key comparison first hashes both the expected and provided keys with SHA-256 before using constant-time comparison (subtle::ConstantTimeEq). This avoids both timing side-channels and length leakage — a pattern that is easy to get wrong. The comment on line 54 explains the rationale concisely.
